category_title: Child with ASD, ADHD, and Specific Learning Disability exhibiting academic resistance and behavioral outbursts
minimal_context: Sam and his tutor are sitting in a quiet spot for a one-to-one lesson to practice identifying letter sounds.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at the letter we learned this morning. Tell me what sound it makes.

STUDENT: I don't want to read.

TUTOR: Just try your best to remember the sound.

STUDENT: I don't know it!

TUTOR: Okay, let's look at this new letter instead. What sound do you think this one makes?

STUDENT: (Pushes the letters off the table and begins to scream) No! I'm not doing it!

options.A: Ask open-ended questions during the one-to-one instructional sessions so that the student can write about what he already knows regarding the letter sounds.
options.B: Implement an extrinsic reinforcement system where the student earns points for attention to task, working hard, and accuracy, pairing specific verbal praise with the points.
options.C: Purposefully select letter-sound materials and reading texts written by and/or featuring people of color to use during the student's one-to-one instructional sessions.
options.D: Encourage the student to bring in items from home that he considers special to use as letter-sound examples during an 'Important Bag' activity.
